title: PEAC Audit Allowlist
description: Time-bounded exceptions for pnpm audit advisories. Each entry must have an expiry date to prevent permanent ignores.

advisory_id.description: The advisory ID from pnpm audit (e.g., GHSA-xxxx-yyyy-zzzz)

package.description: The affected package name (e.g., minimatch, esbuild)

reason.description: Why this advisory is temporarily acceptable

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) after which the exception expires

why_not_exploitable.description: Why this advisory is not exploitable in our usage context

where_used.description: Which surface/package depends on it and how (dev/prod/transitive)

remediation.description: Planned fix (version bump, patch, or replacement)

issue_url.description: Link to tracking issue for remediation

scope.description: Where the vulnerable dependency appears. prod scope has stricter limits (max 30 days, HIGH/CRITICAL never allowed).

dependency_chain.description: Dependency path from root to vulnerable package (e.g., ["jest@30", "test-exclude", "glob", "minimatch@3.1.2"])

verified_by.description: Exact command(s) used to verify the scope and impact (e.g., "pnpm audit --prod shows no HIGH")

added_at.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) when this entry was added

reviewed_at.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) of last renewal review. Required when extending expiry; warns if entry >30 days old without review.
